markdown:
#  Payments Admin Guide 
Table of Contents 
##  View Funding Accounts 
  1. Navigate to the Company level **Payments** tool.
  2. Click the **Payments Settings** ![Icons Settings Gear](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/8d4e4403c0eed73423d93c414c08e3a6/35b17d2e5497c876221891c1d9e5a114/icons-settings-gear.png?fm=webp&h=15) icon. This opens the Payments Settings page. The 'Business Entities' page in the 'Payments Processing' tab is active by default. This page lists all of your company's business entities that have been configured to pay invoices.
  3. For each business entity, click the funding account(s) link to view the account information. 
![Funding Account Link](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/d807fe2bda438595a836a4fb2ddf5930/27261816659839e1c0ca98512eb252ec/funding-account-link.png?fm=webp)


### Funding Accounts Table
The table below details the elements for the Funding Accounts table. [Verified funding accounts](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-is-a-verified-bank-account-in-procore-pay) can receive drawdown requests when [creating disbursements](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/payments-admin-guide/create-disbursements).  
| Nickname  | Field  | A Payments Admin enters the account nickname when linking an account. The nickname allows Payment Disbursers to identify an account when creating disbursements. It also protects your financial privacy by eliminating the need to provide disbursers with the full bank account number.  | [Add Funding Accounts as a Payor](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/about-the-payment-processing-tab-in-the-payments-tool/add-funding-accounts)  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| Verification Status  | Field  | Shows the current verification status of the account.  | [Add Funding Accounts as a Payor](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/about-the-payment-processing-tab-in-the-payments-tool/add-funding-accounts) What are the default verification statuses for bank accounts in Procore Pay?  |  
| Bank ID  | Field  | A Payments Admin enters a unique Bank ID portion to be part of the unique check number issued to the next invoice payout from this account. A payout is an invoice payment in a disbursement.  | [Add Funding Accounts as a Payor](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/about-the-payment-processing-tab-in-the-payments-tool/add-funding-accounts)  |  
| Next Check No.  | Field  | A Payments Admin enters a unique Next Check No to be part of the unique check number issued to the next invoice payout from this account. A payout is an invoice payment in a disbursement.  | [Add Funding Accounts as a Payor](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/about-the-payment-processing-tab-in-the-payments-tool/add-funding-accounts)  |  
| _Default_  | Field  | Indicates the account is the default withdrawal account for Procore Pay in the payor environment.  | [Set a Default Funding Account as a Payor](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/about-the-payment-processing-tab-in-the-payments-tool/set-a-default-funding-account)  |  
| _Deactivated_  | Field  | Indicates the account has been deactivated.  | [Deactivate Funding Accounts as a Payor](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/about-the-payment-processing-tab-in-the-payments-tool/deactivate-accounts)  |  
| Overflow  | Menu  | Click the vertical ellipsis and choose one of the menu options. The available options in this menu are: _Edit_ , _Set as Default Funding Account_ , and _Deactivate_. On the _Default_ account, only _Edit_ and _Deactivate_ are available. On a _Deactivated_ account, only _Edit_ is available.  | [Manage Business Entities as a Payor](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/payor-setup-guide/add-entities)  |  
[ ](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/payments-admin-guide/deactivate-a-funding-account/)
[ Add or Remove Payments Admins ](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/payments-admin-guide/add-or-remove-payments-admins/)
title: Payments Admin Guide - View Funding Accounts
description: This guide provides the payor's Payments Admins with a summary of the actions they can perform with Procore Pay. | View Funding Accounts
